headline: RECORD QUARTER: Agnico Eagle Banks $1.71B Net Income at $4,861 Gold — Sector Sets New Earnings Baseline
snippet: Agnico Eagle reported Q1 2026 adjusted net income of $1.706 billion ($3.41/share) — more than double the prior-year quarter — on a realized gold price of $4,861/oz and 825,109 oz produced, generating $732 million in free cash flow. Record operating margins confirm that $4,800+ gold is now the earnings baseline for well-run senior producers, not an anomaly.

headline: FOUR STRIKES IN 12 MONTHS: Americas Gold Hits 1,392 g/t Silver at Idaho's Galena Complex
snippet: Americas Gold and Silver (TSX: USA) announced its fourth major discovery in 12 months at the Galena Mine in Idaho — the 43L-TJ Vein Complex, comprising six new high-grade silver-copper-antimony veins including 1,392 g/t Ag, 1.5% Cu, and 1.5% Sb over 1.9m. The discovery sits within 25 metres of existing mine infrastructure and remains open for expansion in all directions.

headline: NGEx DRILLS 48m at 7.72% CuEq at Lunahuasi — New High-Grade Zone Found Just 60m Below Surface
snippet: NGEx Minerals (TSX: NGEX) released Phase 4 drill results from its Lunahuasi copper-gold-silver project in San Juan, Argentina: 34m at 7.25% CuEq and 48m at 7.72% CuEq, 180 metres apart within the Mars Zone, plus a brand-new high-grade zone discovered 300m to the east, intersected just 60 metres below surface and open in all directions. Phase 5 drilling and an underground exploration adit are next.

headline: $406M TSX DEBUT: Lumina Metals Triples IPO Target, Eyes Largest Copper-Silver Operation in Europe
snippet: Lumina Metals (TSX: LMCU) raised C$406.2 million in a massively oversubscribed Toronto IPO — triple its original target — backed by mining financier Ross Beaty, whose charitable foundation donated 7.5 million shares to fund environmental initiatives. Shares closed flat at C$12.50. The flagship Nowa Sól project in southwestern Poland hosts an estimated 9 million tonnes of copper and 900 million ounces of silver.

headline: SILVER HOLDS $74 on Dollar Rout — Sixth Consecutive Supply Deficit Firms the Floor
snippet: Silver traded above $74/oz on May 1 after closing April at $74.41 — up 3.72% in the final session — as the U.S. dollar weakened on reported currency intervention in Japan and ongoing Middle East uncertainty. The Silver Institute projects the global market will record its sixth consecutive structural supply deficit in 2026, with a shortfall of approximately 67 million ounces.

headline: CHINA'S SILVER BINGE: 836-Tonne March Import Record Signals Tighter Global Supply as Solar Demand Surges
snippet: China imported 836 tonnes of silver in March 2026 — a new all-time monthly record — driven by retail investment demand and surging solar panel manufacturing. VanEck's weekly Gold SWOT also flags the People's Bank of China's 17th consecutive gold purchase in March, adding five tonnes to reach 2,313 tonnes in total reserves, equal to 9% of China's foreign exchange holdings.

headline: USA RARE EARTH's $2.8B Serra Verde Buy: The Only Non-Asian Source of All Four Magnetic REEs at Scale
snippet: USA Rare Earth (NYSE: USAR) is acquiring Brazil's Serra Verde Group — owner of the Pela Ema mine, the only producer outside Asia capable of supplying all four magnetic rare earths at commercial scale — for $300 million in cash plus 126.8 million USAR shares, implying a $2.8 billion equity value. The deal closes Q3 2026 pending regulatory approval; USAR stock surged 13% on the announcement.

headline: EMPEROR METALS Hits Visible Gold 1.5km East of Resource at Duquesne West — 23,000m Program Underway
snippet: Emperor Metals (CSE: AUOZ) intercepted visible gold in a drill hole 1.5 kilometres east of its established 1.46-million-ounce Inferred Resource at Duquesne West in Quebec's Abitibi Greenstone Belt, materially extending the known mineralized footprint. The ongoing 23,000-metre 2026 program targets resource expansion toward an Indicated category estimate.

headline: COPPER TIGHTENS Near $5.95/lb — Grasberg Force Majeure and Chile Sulphur Crunch Deepen 2026 Deficit
snippet: Copper held near $5.95/lb after five straight sessions of losses, supported by twin supply disruptions: Freeport's Grasberg mine in Indonesia continues to operate below capacity after a fatal mudslide triggered force majeure, while China's curbs on sulphuric acid exports have disrupted refining operations for roughly half of Chile's copper output. Analysts at Benchmark Mineral Intelligence project these shocks will plunge the refined copper market into deficit in 2026.

headline: AGNICO EAGLE LOCKS UP FINLAND: $3.8B Three-Way Sweep Seizes Europe's Largest Undeveloped Gold Deposit
snippet: Agnico Eagle (TSX/NYSE: AEM) announced simultaneous acquisitions of Rupert Resources (C$2.9B for the Ikkari gold deposit — Europe's largest undeveloped gold project), Aurion Resources (C$481M cash), and B2Gold's 70% Fingold stake, consolidating the Central Lapland Greenstone Belt. The 67% premium paid for Rupert reflects the structural scarcity of large, developable gold assets in stable, permitted jurisdictions.

headline: EQUITIES FINALLY CATCHING GOLD: Majors' Record Cash Flow Signals Long-Overdue Miner Re-Rating
snippet: After dramatically underperforming bullion in 2025 — gold rose 60% while GDX gained 35-40% — gold mining equities are showing early signs of a structural re-rating as record operating margins validate higher multiples. BMO Capital Markets raised gold equity price targets an average of 15%, and analysts argue that at $4,500+ sustained gold, earnings leverage to equities has only begun to be priced in.

headline: DEAL SURGE: Mining M&A Hits $21.6B in Q1 2026 — Strongest Start Since 2023 as Majors Race to Restock Reserves
snippet: Global mining mergers and acquisitions reached $21.6 billion across 121 transactions in Q1 2026 — the strongest start to a year since 2023 — building on $93.7 billion in full-year 2025 deals, the highest total in 13 years. Gold and critical minerals are driving activity as senior producers exhaust organic reserve replacement options and look to bolt-on acquisitions in friendly jurisdictions.
